Justin Poon 07/12/2021 0.1 Short Ctrl key added to Item Prompt (Scan Equals Bundle Quantity) Justin Poon 7/23/2021 0.2 Single GS1-128 barcode scan per LPN regardless of standard LPN quantity Brianne Williams 08/17/2022 0.3 Catch weight for every unit Tim Fischer 10/18/2022 0. 3 Changed REF_NUM5 = 2,3,4,5 requirements Brianne Williams 10/18/2022 0.3 CTRL-L skip item prompt CR Brianne Williams 1/ 29 /2025 1.0 Retail FSMA updates EX55 – Pick from Reserve Traceability Capture For export and redistribution orders, McLane Foodservice picks from reserve locations. Per customer requirements, certain items are designated as traceability capture required and require a GS1-128 barcode scan to capture lot and/or catch weight information. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Pull iLPN List Reporting N Other N EX55 Pick from Reserve Traceability Capture enables attribute tracking based on order line level custom flag. The base RF Pull iLPN List transaction is customized to prompt for GS1-128 capture if the task is designated as traceability capture required based on the order line indicator. When the task is flagged as traceability capture required, WMOS requires a single scan to capture the required traceability information and pack the oLPN. In the scenario that lot capture is required but lot AI is not available in the barcode, WMOS defaults the batch number field with a default value. If catch weight capture is required but catch weight AI is not available in the barcode, WMOS prompts the user to manually enter a catch weight. In addition to AI capture, WMOS saves the captured barcode in an LPN detail table column. Assumptions No customization to the oLPN shipping label which is printed in this process is in scope for this extension. EX55 INT2 Pick from Reserve tasks are for a single oLPN at a time. An EX55 INT2 task is either traceable capture required or nontraceable capture required. An order cannot have multiple order lines for the same item with different traceability capture requirements. EX55 is supported for full and partial pull INT2 tasks. LPNs picked through EX55 are single SKU and single lot iLPNs only. RF Anchor post packing is supported by EX55. Substitution, alternate, and other base RF Pull iLPN List Pull-From iLPN Prompt ctrl key logic is not affected by EX55 logic and works per base. Item and Item attributes prompt during partial pulling is not supported in EX55 RF Pull List iLPN flow. Catch weight tolerance is not performed if catch weight is captured from GS1 scan. A single shipping label is printed for each pallet for the pick to reserve flow. A single GS1-128 barcode scan is required for an LPN regardless of if the LPN is a full standard pallet quantity or not. Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement Distribution Order DistributionOrder à LineItem à ReferenceNumberField 5 ‘5’ – Sent to identify the order line requires lot and avg catch weight tracking ‘4’ – Sent to identify the order line requires avg catch weight tracking ‘3’ – Sent to identify the order line requires lot and catch weight tracking ‘2’ – Sent to identify the order line requires catch weight tracking ‘1’ – Sent to identify the order line requires lot tracking ‘0’ or NULL – Sent to identify the order line does NOT require lot or catch weight tracking ITEM_WMS.BATCH_REQD = ‘ 1’ identifies that the item requires lot tracking. Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram ￼ Process Steps: Users initiate work in the appropriate task group by requesting work from WMOS through CTRL-S from the RF main menu to request a pick from reserve picking task. WMOS directs the user to the location and prompts the user to confirm the iLPN. If a partial LPN is pulled per task or Ctrl-L is invoke d to short the pull, then WMOS prompts the user to confirm the quantity. WMOS determines if the task is traceability capture required based on the ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5 value. If the task is traceability capture required, continue to step 5. If the task is not traceability capture required, proceed per base. WMOS prompts for a single GS1-128 barcode capture per LPN . If the task is catch weight capture required, determine if catch weight AI was captured in GS1-128 barcode scan. If task is not catch weight capture required or catch weight was captured, then proceed to parse AI values to the LPN_DETAIL and LPN_CATCH_WEIGHT table. Otherwise prompt for manual catch weight entry. Proceed per base INT2 task action configuration. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF Pull iLPN List Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI (RF UI) Y Mobile (DM) N Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX55 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX55 is turned off, and no custom EX55 processing will occur. Lastly, the custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. If custom transaction parameter 502-502 is set to “Y””, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled. If custom transaction parameter 502-502 is “N” or not setup at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues per base. Screen 1 – Pull from iLPN Prompt The base RF Pull iLPN List Pull from iLPN Prompt is enhanced to determine if the task is designated as traceability capture required. A task is designated as traceability capture required if the order line for the task detail is designated as traceability capture required (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5= 1, 2, 3,4,or 5 ). The user is prompted to scan GS1-128 barcode if the task detail is designated as a traceability order line. Display Not required for this extension . This screen displays per base. Ctrl Keys Not required for this extension. All Ctrl keys are as per base given custom functionality is invoked after pulling . Validations Not required for this extension . All validations are per base. Updates Not required for this extension . All updates are per base. Navigation Upon scanning a valid iLPN or entering quantity ( partial pull mode ), WM determines is the task is designated as traceability capture required. The user is navigated to the item prompt to scan a GS1-128 barcode to capture traceability information for the LPN if the task is traceability capture required. A task is designated as traceability capture required if ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5= 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 . IF task is partial pull mode THEN navigate to the Quantity Prompt IF task is traceability capture required (SELECT REF_NUM5 FROM ORDER_LINE_ITEM OLI JOIN TASK_DTL TD ON OLI.LINE_ITEM_ID = TD.LINE_ITEM_ID WHERE TASK_ID = {Current Task} AND TASK_SEQ_NBR = {Current Task Detail Sequence Number}) IN (1,2,3 ,4,5 ) THEN navigate to the Item Prompt ELSE proceed per base flow. Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No chases to base labor hooks as part of this extension. Screen 2 - Quantity Prompt The base RF Pull iLPN List Quantity Prompt displays to the user if the task is partial pull mode . Upon entering quantity, WM determines if the task is designated as traceability capture required (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5= 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 ). The user is prompted to scan GS1-128 barcode if the task detail is designated as a traceability order line. Display Not required for this extension . This screen displays per base. Ctrl Keys Not required for this extension. All Ctrl keys are as per base given custom functionality is invoked after pulling . Validations WM performs base quantity entry validations. IF the user enters a quantity less than quantity allocated, The SYSTEM WARNING 1670, “The quantity entered is less than the allocated qty”, displays. The User can Ctrl-A to accpt/proceed from this warning or Ctrl-W to back to enter different quantity Updates Not required for this extension . All updates are per base. Navigation Upon entering quantity ( partial pull mode ), WM determines i f the task is designated as traceability capture required. The user is navigated to the item prompt to scan a GS1-128 barcode to capture traceability information for the LPN if the task is traceability capture required. A task is designated as traceability capture required if ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5= 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 . IF task is traceability capture required , but not catch weight (SELECT REF_NUM5 FROM ORDER_LINE_ITEM OLI JOIN TASK_DTL TD ON OLI.LINE_ITEM_ID = TD.LINE_ITEM_ID WHERE TASK_ID = {Current Task} AND TASK_SEQ_NBR = {Current Task Detail Sequence Number}) IN (1 ,4,5 ) THEN navigate to Item Prompt for one sca n IF task is traceability with catch weight re quired (SELECT REF_NUM5 FROM ORDER_LINE_ITEM OLI JOIN TASK_DTL TD ON OLI.LINE_ITEM_ID = TD.LINE_ITEM_ID WHERE TASK_ID = {Current Task} AND TASK_SEQ_NBR = {Current Task Detail Sequence Number}) IN ( 2,3) Then navigate to the Item Promp t for the number of quantity entered . The user will be prompted for the item Prompt for each quantity that was ent ered, followed by a catch weight prompt if not captured on the GS1 barcode scan. ELSE proceed per base flow. Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to base activity tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks No chases to base labor hooks as part of this extension. Screen 2 – Item Prompt This screen displays if the task is designated as traceability capture required. The user is prompted to scan a GS1-128 barcode to capture all traceability information to pass onto the oLPN . In Scenario’s where Catch Weight is required for each unit ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5= 2 or 3) the user will be prompted to the Item Prompt for the number of quantity they are picking. Ctrl-X CTRLKEY/1001 Exit Exit Ctrl-L CTRLKEY No Barcode Skips the item barcode screen ; proceeds to next screen in flow Validations WMOS performs the base validations for Item confirmation. If any of these validations fail, the base error displays and no custom logic is required. WMOS also performs custom validation to identify if the scanned barcode is populated with EAN prefix configured for the warehouse. If not, WMOS displays custom warning message CUST/5502 “Invalid GS1-128 barcode”. Otherwise, WMOS proceeds with GS1-128 barcode parsing and validations per base and captures following AI values in session. Application Identifier Format Description 00 18 byte – Fixed Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC). This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 01 14 byte – Fixed Item GTIN (Global Trade Item Number). This field is used for validation since it’s already configured as WMOS item master XREF barcode. 10 20 byte – Variable Lot/batch number. 11 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) Manufacturing date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 13 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) Packaging Date. 15 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) Sell by /Ship by date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 17 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) Expiry date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 310 6 – byte – Variable Catch Weight (kgs) 320 6 byte – Variable Catch Weight (lbs) 422 3 bytes – Fixed Country of Origin. Updates If the order line is designated as catch weight capture required (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5 = 2 or 3), then validate that catch weight AI is captured in the barcode scanned. If catch weight is required but not captured in the barcode scanned, then defer all updates until Catch Weight Prompt. If catch weight is captured as part of the barcode scanned or catch weight is not required, then proceed with the following updates. Upon successful Item Scan, WMOS performs base packing updates through INT2 task action completion program configured. In addition to the packing updates, WMOS save s all base AI values parsed into LPN detail and LPN catch weight table. This includes some base AIs not supported for RF Pull iLPN List . In addition to AI capture, WMOS save s the scanned GS1-128 barcode in the LPN detail table column LPN_DETAIL. REF_FIELD_ 7 . The following table details the AI fields supported by base. Application Identifier Format Database Table Column Description 00 18 byte – Fixed LPN_DETAIL. REF_FIELD_1 Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC). This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 10 20 byte – Variable LPN_DETAIL. BATCH_NBR Lot/batch number. (If lot capture is required (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5 = 1 or 3) but not captured, then update LPN_DETAIL.BATCH_NBR = ‘0000000000’) 11 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) LPN_DETAIL.MANUFACTURED_DTTM Manufacturing date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 13 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) LPN_DETAIL.REF_FIELD_2 Packaging Date. 15 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) LPN_DETAIL. SHIP_BY_DATE Sell by /Ship by date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 17 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) LPN_DETAIL. EXPIRATION_DATE Expiry date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 310 6 byte – Variable Base LPN_CATCH_WEIGHT table insert Catch Weight (kg). The catch weight must be converted to lbs. 320 6 byte – Variable Base LPN_CATCH_WEIGHT table insert Catch Weight (lbs). 422 3 bytes – Fixed LPN_DETAIL. CNTRY_OF_ORGN Country of Origin. Tech Note1: LPN_DETAIL. REF_FIELD_ 7 column is modified through EX08 to VARCHAR2(100) to save the scanned GS1-128 barcode. Navigation WM validates whether manual catch weight entry is required. IF the order line is designated as catch weight capture required (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5 = 2 or 3), then validate that catch weight AI is included in the barcode scanned. IF catch weight AI is not captured as part of the barcode scan or CTRL-L was invoked THEN navigate to the Catch Weight Prompt screen ELSE upon successfully capturing the item, after packing completion WMOS navigates to the next screen as defined in INT2/Task Action/Task Path configuration. ELSE upon successfully capturing the item, after packing completion WMOS navigates to the next screen as defined in INT2/Task Action/Task Path configuration. Screen 4 – Catch Weight Prompt The Catch Weight Prompt displays to prompt the user to capture the catch weight for the LPN if the task is designated as catch weight required, but catch weight was not captured as part of the barcode scanned either because the GS1-128 barcode did not have a catch weight AI, only a GTIN barcode was available, or no barcode is available and item name is entered instead , or CTRL-L was invoked . This screen does not prompt if the task is designated as an average weight catch weight. Updates Upon successful catch weight entry, WMOS save s all base AI values parsed into LPN detail table. This includes some base AIs not supported for RF Pull iLPN List . In addition to AI capture, WMOS save s the scanned GS1-128 barcode in the LPN detail table column LPN_DETAIL. REF_FIELD_ 7 . WMOS moves to the next task detail, if multiple iLPNs are on a task. SHIP_BY_DATE Sell by /Ship by date. This is LPN header information but customized to capture for LPN detail. 17 6 byte – Fixed ( YYMMDD) LPN_DETAIL. EXPIRATION_DATE Expiry date. Justin Poon 07/12/2021 0.1 Short Ctrl key added to Item Prompt (Scan Equals Bundle Quantity) Justin Poon 7/23/2021 0.2 Single GS1-128 barcode s can per LPN regardless of standard LPN quantity EX55 – Pick from Reserve Traceability Capture For export and redistribution orders, McLane Foodservice picks from reserve locations. Per customer requirements, certain items are designated as traceability capture required and require a GS1-128 barcode scan to capture lot and/or catch weight information. There are different traceability scan capture requirements depending on if the pallet picked is for a full standard quantity iLPN or non-full standard quantity iLPN. For full standard quantity iLPNs. A single scan per pallet is required to capture all the required traceability information. For non-full standard quantity iLPNs, a single scan per pack/unit is required to capture all the required traceability information. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Pull iLPN List Reporting N Other N EX55 Pick from Reserve Traceability Capture enables attribute tracking based on order line level custom flag. The base RF Pull iLPN List transaction is customized to prompt for GS1-128 capture if the task is designated as traceability capture required based on the order line indicator. When the task is flagged as traceability capture required, WMOS evaluates if the iLPN pulled is for a full standard quantity. If the iLPN pulled is for a full standard iLPN quantity, WMOS requires a single scan to capture the required traceability information and pack the oLPN. If the iLPN pull is not for a full standard iLPN quantity, WMOS requires a scan per pack/unit to capture the required traceability information and pull each individual pack/unit. In the scenario that lot capture is required but lot AI is not available in the barcode, WMOS defaults the batch number field with a default value. If catch weight capture is required but catch weight AI is not available in the barcode, WMOS prompts the user to manually enter a catch weight. In addition to AI capture, WMOS saves the captured barcode in an LPN detail table column. Assumptions No customization to the oLPN shipping label which is printed in this process is in scope for this extension. EX55 INT2 Pick from Reserve tasks are for a single oLPN at a time. An EX55 INT2 task is either traceable capture required or nontraceable capture required. An order cannot have multiple order lines for the same item with different traceability capture requirements. EX55 is supported for full and partial pull INT2 tasks. LPNs picked through EX55 are single SKU and single lot iLPNs only. RF Anchor post packing is supported by EX55. Substitution, alternate, and other base RF Pull iLPN List Pull-From iLPN Prompt ctrl key logic is not affected by EX55 logic and works per base. Item and Item attributes prompt during partial pulling is not supported in EX55 RF Pull List iLPN flow. Catch weight tolerance is not performed if catch weight is captured from GS1 scan. A single shipping label is printed for each pallet for the pick to reserve flow, regardless of whether a full pallet or partial pallet is pulled. . A single GS1-128 barcode scan is required for an LPN regardless of if the LPN is a full standard pallet quantity or not. If task detail quantity is shorted at the Item Prompt (Scan Equals Bundle Quantity) screen, the shorted quantity is removed from WMOS inventory and inventory PIXes are sent to the host. Justin Poon 07/12/2021 0.1 Short Ctrl key added to Item Prompt (Scan Equals Bundle Quantity) Justin Poon 7/23/2021 0.2 Single GS1-128 barcode scan per LPN regardless of standard LPN quantity Brianne Williams 08/17/2022 0.3 Catch weight for every unit Tim Fischer 10/18/2022 0. 3 Changed REF_NUM5 = 2,3,4,5 requirements Brianne Williams 10/18/2022 0.3 CTRL-L skip item prompt CR Brianne Williams 1/ 29 /2025 1.0 Retail FSMA updates EX55 – Pick from Reserve Traceability Capture For export and redistribution orders, McLane Foodservice picks from reserve locations. Per customer requirements, certain items are designated as traceability capture required and require a GS1-128 barcode scan to capture lot and/or catch weight information. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF Pull iLPN List Reporting N Other N EX55 Pick from Reserve Traceability Capture enables attribute tracking based on order line level custom flag. The base RF Pull iLPN List transaction is customized to prompt for GS1-128 capture if the task is designated as traceability capture required based on the order line indicator.

[Document: MCLN EX55 Pick from Reserve Traceability Capture]

g N Other N EX55 Pick from Reserve Traceability Capture enables attribute tracking based on order line level custom flag. The base RF Pull iLPN List transaction is customized to prompt for GS1-128 capture if the task is designated as traceability capture required based on the order line indicator.

When the task is flagged as traceability capture required, WMOS requires a single scan to capture the required traceability information and pack the oLPN. In the scenario that lot capture is required but lot AI is not available in the barcode, WMOS defaults the batch number field with a default value. If catch weight capture is required but catch weight AI is not available in the barcode, WMOS prompts the user to manually enter a catch weight. In addition to AI capture, WMOS saves the captured barcode in an LPN detail table column. Assumptions No customization to the oLPN shipping label which is printed in this process is in scope for this extension. EX55 INT2 Pick from Reserve tasks are for a single oLPN at a time. An EX55 INT2 task is either traceable capture required or nontraceable capture required. An order cannot have multiple order lines for the same item with different traceability capture requirements. EX55 is supported for full and partial pull INT2 tasks. LPNs picked through EX55 are single SKU and single lot iLPNs only. RF Anchor post packing is supported by EX55. Substitution, alternate, and other base RF Pull iLPN List Pull-From iLPN Prompt ctrl key logic is not affected by EX55 logic and works per base. Item and Item attributes prompt during partial pulling is not supported in EX55 RF Pull List iLPN flow. Catch weight tolerance is not performed if catch weight is captured from GS1 scan. A single shipping label is printed for each pallet for the pick to reserve flow. A single GS1-128 barcode scan is required for an LPN regardless of if the LPN is a full standard pallet quantity or not. Platform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Process Flow Diagram ￼ Process Steps: Users initiate work in the appropriate task group by requesting work from WMOS through CTRL-S from the RF main menu to request a pick from reserve picking task. WMOS directs the user to the location and prompts the user to confirm the iLPN. If a partial LPN is pulled per task or Ctrl-L is invoke d to short the pull, then WMOS prompts the user to confirm the quantity. WMOS determines if the task is traceability capture required based on the ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5 value. If the task is traceability capture required, continue to step 5. If the task is not traceability capture required, proceed per base. WMOS prompts for a single GS1-128 barcode capture per LPN . If the task is catch weight capture required, determine if catch weight AI was captured in GS1-128 barcode scan. If task is not catch weight capture required or catch weight was captured, then proceed to parse AI values to the LPN_DETAIL and LPN_CATCH_WEIGHT table. Otherwise prompt for manual catch weight entry. Proceed per base INT2 task action configuration. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF Pull iLPN List Program Platform Available Text Based (RF Gun) Y UI (RF UI) Y Mobile (DM) N Enablement The following section describes the custom configuration required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX55 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX55 is turned off, and no custom EX55 processing will occur. Lastly, the custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. If custom transaction parameter 502-502 is set to “Y””, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled.

he flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX55 is turned off, and no custom EX55 processing will occur. Lastly, the custom RF transaction parameter must be enabled. If custom transaction parameter 502-502 is set to “Y””, then the custom functionality within this section is enabled.

If custom transaction parameter 502-502 is “N” or not setup at all, WMOS exits custom logic and continues per base. Screen 1 – Pull from iLPN Prompt The base RF Pull iLPN List Pull from iLPN Prompt is enhanced to determine if the task is designated as traceability capture required. A task is designated as traceability capture required if the order line for the task detail is designated as traceability capture required (ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5= 1, 2, 3,4,or 5 ). The user is prompted to scan GS1-128 barcode if the task detail is designated as a traceability order line. Display Not required for this extension . This screen displays per base. Ctrl Keys Not required for this extension. All Ctrl keys are as per base given custom functionality is invoked after pulling . Validations Not required for this extension . All validations are per base. Updates Not required for this extension . All updates are per base. Navigation Upon scanning a valid iLPN or entering quantity ( partial pull mode ), WM determines is the task is designated as traceability capture required. The user is navigated to the item prompt to scan a GS1-128 barcode to capture traceability information for the LPN if the task is traceability capture required. A task is designated as traceability capture required if ORDER_LINE_ITEM.REF_NUM5= 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 . IF task is partial pull mode THEN navigate to the Quantity Prompt IF task is traceability capture required (SELECT REF_NUM5 FROM ORDER_LINE_ITEM OLI JOIN TASK_DTL TD ON OLI.LINE_ITEM_ID = TD.LINE_ITEM_ID WHERE TASK_ID = {Current Task} AND TASK_SEQ_NBR = {Current Task Detail Sequence Number}) IN (1,2,3 ,4,5 ) THEN navigate to the Item Prompt ELSE proceed per base flow. Justin Poon 07/12/2021 0.1 Short Ctrl key added to Item Prompt (Scan Equals Bundle Quantity) Justin Poon 7/23/2021 0.2 Single GS1-128 barcode s can per LPN regardless of standard LPN quantity EX55 – Pick from Reserve Traceability Capture For export and redistribution orders, McLane Foodservice picks from reserve locations. Per customer requirements, certain items are designated as traceability capture required and require a GS1-128 barcode scan to capture lot and/or catch weight information. There are different traceability scan capture requirements depending on if the pallet picked is for a full standard quantity iLPN or non-full standard quantity iLPN. For full standard quantity iLPNs. A single scan per pallet is required to capture all the required traceability information. For non-full standard quantity iLPNs, a single scan per pack/unit is required to capture all the required traceability information. The base RF Pull iLPN List transaction is customized to prompt for GS1-128 capture if the task is designated as traceability capture required based on the order line indicator. When the task is flagged as traceability capture required, WMOS evaluates if the iLPN pulled is for a full standard quantity. If the iLPN pulled is for a full standard iLPN quantity, WMOS requires a single scan to capture the required traceability information and pack the oLPN. If the iLPN pull is not for a full standard iLPN quantity, WMOS requires a scan per pack/unit to capture the required traceability information and pull each individual pack/unit. In the scenario that lot capture is required but lot AI is not available in the barcode, WMOS defaults the batch number field with a default value. If catch weight capture is required but catch weight AI is not available in the barcode, WMOS prompts the user to manually enter a catch weight. In addition to AI capture, WMOS saves the captured barcode in an LPN detail table column. Assumptions No customization to the oLPN shipping label which is printed in this process is in scope for this extension. EX55 INT2 Pick from Reserve tasks are for a single oLPN at a time. An EX55 INT2 task is either traceable capture required or nontraceable capture required. An order cannot have multiple order lines for the same item with different traceability capture requirements. EX55 is supported for full and partial pull INT2 tasks. LPNs picked through EX55 are single SKU and single lot iLPNs only. RF Anchor post packing is supported by EX55. Substitution, alternate, and other base RF Pull iLPN List Pull-From iLPN Prompt ctrl key logic is not affected by EX55 logic and works per base. Item and Item attributes prompt during partial pulling is not supported in EX55 RF Pull List iLPN flow. Catch weight tolerance is not performed if catch weight is captured from GS1 scan. A single shipping label is printed for each pallet for the pick to reserve flow, regardless of whether a full pallet or partial pallet is pulled. . A single GS1-128 barcode scan is required for an LPN regardless of if the LPN is a full standard pallet quantity or not. If task detail quantity is shorted at the Item Prompt (Scan Equals Bundle Quantity) screen, the shorted quantity is removed from WMOS inventory and inventory PIXes are sent to the host.
